away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Jordan Horston
players.injury_comment: Horston is battling a foot injury, which kept her off the floor for Wednesday's 94-89 loss to Portland. Jade Melbourne entered the starting lineup for that contest and is the favorite to remain with the first five for Saturday's meeting with the Mercury.

players.name: Ezi Magbegor
players.injury_comment: Magbegor sustained her injury while playing for Australia in the 2026 FIBA Basketball World Cup Qualifiers, and she appears to be on track to miss the start of the WNBA regular season as a result. The 26-year-old has played a prominent role in Seattle's frontcourt across the past several seasons, most recently averaging 8.0 points, 6.2 rebounds, 2.2 blocks and 2.1 assists in 27.3 minutes per game while starting every regular-season matchup in 2025, while her absence to begin the 2026 season could lead to increased opportunities for Dominique Malonga, Stefanie Dolson and rookie Awa Fam.

players.name: Taina Mair
players.injury_comment: Mair is set to sit out Saturday's contest in order to preserve her eligibility while signed to a developmental contract. Her next chance to play will come Monday against Dallas.

injuries.name: Taina Mair
injuries.comment: Mair is set to sit out Saturday's contest in order to preserve her eligibility while signed to a developmental contract. Her next chance to play will come Monday against Dallas.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Monique Akoa Makani
players.injury_comment: Akoa Makani will miss her first game since May 24 due to a left hamstring injury. In her absence, Alexa Held is a leading candidate to slide into the starting lineup. Akoa Makani's next opportunity to suit up will come Monday against Indiana.

players.name: Jovana Nogic
players.injury_comment: Nogic recently scored 12 points across 21 minutes in Wednesday's 86-76 loss to the Aces. She is not with the team, forcing her to miss her first game of the 2026 campaign, meaning Alexa Held could see more action from the bench in Saturday's clash with the Storm.

players.name: Marta Suarez
players.injury_comment: Suarez will be inactive as Phoenix looks to preserve her eligibility as a developmental player. She has appeared in three games this season, and her next opportunity to suit up will come Monday against Indiana.

players.name: Sami Whitcomb
players.injury_comment: This is a tough blow for Whitcomb and the Mercury as she will now miss the first month or so of the WNBA season. Whitcomb served as a part-time starter during her first season in Phoenix last year, averaging 9.1 points, 2.6 rebounds and 2.5 assists in 23.9 minutes per game across 43 regular-season appearances (20 starts). Kiana Williams could see an uptick in minutes with this news.
